text: **Anna:** Wow.
**Anna:** Did you know that {bees|ミツバチ} are dying?
**Kaveh:** That is bad news.
**Kaveh:** If all the {bees|ミツバチ} died, there'd be no food.
**Kaveh:** Bees are very {important|重要な}.
**Anna:** Yes, they are.
**Anna:** Speaking of bees, one of our little {friends|友達} is here now.
**Anna:** You know, Anna, if you {ignore|無視する} it, it will fly away.
**Anna:** But if you {swat|叩き払う} it, it might {sting|刺す} you.
**Anna:** I'll ignore it.
**Anna:** I can ignore it
**Anna:** See?
**Anna:** I'm ignoring it.
**Kaveh:** Anna, are you {afraid|恐れている} of bees?
**Anna:** What makes you say that?
**Kaveh:** Anna, you should call my friend Caroline.
**Kaveh:** She's a {beekeeper|養蜂家} and a bee {educator|教育者}.
**Anna:** If you talk to her, she'll probably help you get over your {fear|恐怖} of bees.
**Anna:** I'm not afraid of bees!
**Anna:** Here's your card
**Anna:** In this lesson, you can learn about {conditionals|条件文}.
**Anna:** They have if, and will, or would.
**Anna:** There are two kinds of conditional sentences in this lesson
**Anna:** Type 1 conditionals have a real event and a result that probably will happen.
**Anna:** Chances are good.
**Kaveh:** Here is the pattern.
**Anna:** If plus present tense verb will plus future tense verb.
**Anna:** Kave uses this when he says if you ignore it, it will fly away.
**Anna:** Type II conditionals have a possible event and a result that may or may not happen
**Anna:** We don't know.
**Kaveh:** Here is the {pattern|パターン/模型|/ˈpætərn/|This is a nice pattern.}
**Anna:** If plus {past tense|過去形} verb, would plus {infinitive|不定詞} verb.
**Kaveh:** Kaveh uses this pattern when he says, if all the {bees|蜂|/biːz/|Bees make honey.} died, there'd be no food.
**Anna:** Look for the if in the sentence to find more {conditionals|条件文} in today's lesson.
**Anna:** I'll color them too
**Anna:** I think I'm at the wrong {address|住所|/əˈdres/|This is my home address.}
**Anna:** This does not look like a place where bees are {kept|保管される|/kept/|We kept the book on the shelf.}
**Anna:** Oh, sorry to {bother|邪魔する/煩わせる|/ˈbɑːðər/|Don't bother me now.} you.
**Anna:** I'm looking for {beekeeper|養蜂家} Caroline.
**Anna:** I'm here to take her
**Anna:** Learn to love bees class.
**Caroline:** You're in the right place.
**Kaveh:** I'm Caroline.
**Anna:** Oh nice to meet you.
**Anna:** Um, Caroline, I thought this class used real bees and real {hives|巣箱|/haɪvz/|Bees live in hives.}
**Caroline:** It does.
**Anna:** If you look in my {backyard|裏庭}, you will see my bees.
**Kaveh:** I'm a home {beekeeper|養蜂家}.
**Anna:** What?
**Anna:** You live with bees?
**Anna:** That is really {amazing|驚くべき|/əˈmeɪzɪŋ/|This view is amazing.}
**Anna:** And at the same time really scary.
**Caroline:** It's not scary.
**Anna:** If you come, I'll show you.
**Anna:** Caroline, how many bees are in that hive?
**Caroline:** About 30,000 in each.
**Anna:** What?
**Anna:** That's amazing.
**Anna:** But aren't you {afraid|恐れている|/əˈfreɪd/|I am afraid of dogs.} they're gonna come out and kill you?
**Caroline:** Not at all, Anna.
**Caroline:** Even my {children|子供たち} help me.
**Caroline:** Tell me, why are you {afraid|恐れている} of bees?
**Anna:** I don't know
**Anna:** Well, when I was a little girl, my mom ran out of {honey|はちみつ}, and I really wanted honey for my {pancakes|パンケーキ}.
**Anna:** So I {climbed|登った} high into a tree and hit a {beehive|蜂の巣} with a stick several times.
**Anna:** Then I reached inside with my {bare|むき出しの} hands to pull the honey out.
**Anna:** That's when I got {stung|刺された} a lot
**Anna:** Caroline, if you hit a beehive with a stick and reach inside, you might get stung.
**Anna:** No, Anna, if you do those things, you will get stung.
**Anna:** Yes, I did.
**Caroline:** And you know that it was your fault you got stung, don't you?
**Anna:** Yes, I did.
**Caroline:** Bees know your {feelings|感情}.
**Anna:** And if you stay calm, the bees will be calm.
**Kaveh:** If you're {nervous|緊張した}, the bees will be nervous.
**Anna:** And if bees are nervous, they may sting.
**Anna:** So please be calm.
**Anna:** I will be calm.
**Anna:** I am calm.
**Anna:** Okay, I'm ready.
**Anna:** I'm ready.
**Caroline:** Great, let's get our {equipment|装備} on.
**Anna:** Wait, wait, I'm not ready.
**Anna:** I'm not calm.
**Anna:** I'm nervous.
**Anna:** Give me one minute to be calm.
**Anna:** Are you ready for one more {conditional|条件の} sentence?
**Anna:** If you are not afraid, you will join us next time to learn more about bees.
